_**User**_

Search Screen
Reference Designs:https://github.com/NaveenSinghTTN/ai-first-dev-training-mobile/blob/main/phase-2/docs/design/screens/search_default.png   https://github.com/NaveenSinghTTN/ai-first-dev-training-mobile/blob/main/phase-2/docs/design/screens/search_results.png 

State 1 — Default (No Query)
Header: StreamList wordmark + circular user avatar (top right, placeholder icon)

Search Bar

surface_container_low background, 12px radius
Magnifier icon left, placeholder: "Search movies, actors, directors..."
Focused state: subtle outline_variant ghost border at 15% opacity
Genre Quick Filter Chips

Horizontally scrollable below the search bar
Same chip style as Home. Tapping pre-populates the search bar and immediately triggers a search.
Recent Searches

Section title "Recent Searches" (headline-md) + "CLEAR ALL" right-aligned (primary_container, title-sm uppercase)
Up to 5 entries. Each: clock icon + search term (body-md, on_surface)
Stored and retrieved from AsyncStorage
Shown only when search bar is empty / not focused
Trending Now Section

One large featured landscape card with "FEATURED" badge (secondary_container), title (title-lg), genre • year • runtime in on_surface_variant
2-column portrait grid below for additional trending titles, each showing rating badge
State 2 — Results
Search bar with active query text
Result count: {n} results for '{query}' in on_surface_variant label-sm
2-column portrait grid: poster, title (title-lg), year (label-sm, on_surface_variant), rating badge top-right
Zero results state: centred icon, "No results for '{query}'" (headline-md), suggestion copy (body-md, on_surface_variant)
Interactions
Debounced search: API call fires 400ms after the user stops typing. Implement with useRef + clearTimeout. Do not fire on every keystroke.

Request cancellation (required, not stretch): If a new keystroke arrives before the previous call resolves, cancel the outstanding request using Axios CancelToken or AbortController. This prevents stale results from overwriting newer ones.

Tapping a result navigates to DetailScreen
Recent searches saved on each completed search (not on each keystroke)
"Clear All" removes all recent searches from AsyncStorage and clears the list immediately
Minimum Bar
Search functional with results in 2-column grid. Navigation to Detail working.

What Good Looks Like
Debounce correctly implemented. Request cancellation demonstrably working (verify by rapid typing). Recent searches persisted across app restarts. Genre chips pre-populate and trigger correctly. Zero-results state handled.



---

  "name": "Search Screen Implementation",
  "overview": "Implement the full Search tab per spec: nested stack for Detail navigation, TMDB search API with debounced requests and AbortSignal cancellation, AsyncStorage-backed recent searches, default trending UI with featured + grid, and results/zero states—all data in hooks/components under existing architecture rules.",
  "plan": "# Search Screen Implementation Plan\n\n## Current gaps\n\n- [&#96;SearchScreen.tsx&#96;](src/screens/SearchScreen.tsx) and [&#96;useSearch.ts&#96;](src/hooks/useSearch.ts) are placeholders; Search tab has **no stack**, so it cannot push [&#96;DetailScreen&#96;](src/screens/DetailScreen.tsx) while staying on the Search tab (unlike [&#96;HomeStackNavigator&#96;](src/navigation/RootNavigator.tsx)).\n- [&#96;src/api/client.ts&#96;](src/api/client.ts) &#96;getJson&#96; does not accept cancellation; TMDB search is not implemented in [&#96;movies.ts&#96;](src/api/movies.ts).\n- Genre UI can mirror [&#96;GenreFilterStrip&#96;](src/components/home/GenreFilterStrip.tsx) styles; Search-specific behavior (chip tap = immediate search, **no** debounce) differs from Home’s selection model.\n\n## Architecture (aligned with project rules)\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;mermaid\nflowchart TB\n  subgraph hooks [src/hooks]\n    useSearchQuery[useSearchQuery / useSearch]\n    useSearchTrending[useSearchTrending]\n    recentStore[recentSearches store]\n  end\n  subgraph api [src/api]\n    client[client.getJson + signal]\n    searchApi[fetchSearchMoviesPage]\n  end\n  SearchScreen[SearchScreen presentation only]\n  useSearchQuery --\u003e client\n  searchApi --\u003e client\n  useSearchQuery --\u003e recentStore\n  useSearchTrending --\u003e searchApi\n  SearchScreen --\u003e useSearchQuery\n  SearchScreen --\u003e useSearchTrending\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n- **Screens**: [&#96;SearchScreen&#96;](src/screens/SearchScreen.tsx) only composes UI and handlers; **no** &#96;useEffect&#96; + API calls in the screen file—all fetching in hooks.\n- **API**: All HTTP via [&#96;createApiClient&#96;](src/api/client.ts); new search function in [&#96;movies.ts&#96;](src/api/movies.ts) only.\n\n## 1. API client: abortable requests\n\nUpdate [&#96;src/api/client.ts&#96;](src/api/client.ts):\n\n- Extend &#96;getJson\u003cT\u003e(path, options?: { signal?: AbortSignal })&#96; and pass &#96;signal&#96; into &#96;instance.get(...)&#96;.\n- In the hook layer, treat cancellation as a **no-op** (do not set error state): for axios v1, check &#96;axios.isCancel(err)&#96; or &#96;err.code === 'ERR_CANCELED'&#96; / &#96;CanceledError&#96; per [axios docs](https://axios-http.com/docs/cancellation).\n\n## 2. TMDB search + types\n\nAdd in [&#96;src/api/movies.ts&#96;](src/api/movies.ts):\n\n- &#96;fetchSearchMoviesPage(query: string, page: number, signal?: AbortSignal)&#96; → &#96;GET /search/movie?query=...\u0026page=...&#96; (encode query).\n- Return mapped list items suitable for UI: reuse [&#96;MovieSummary&#96;](src/api/types.ts) where possible; TMDB search results include **&#96;release_date&#96;** (not on current &#96;TmdbMovieResult&#96;). Add a narrow type for search rows (e.g. &#96;MovieSummary \u0026 { release_date: string | null }&#96; or a dedicated &#96;SearchMovieHit&#96;) and map in &#96;movies.ts&#96; so the grid can show **year** without abusing &#96;any&#96;.\n\nReuse [&#96;PaginatedMoviesResponse&#96;](src/api/types.ts) if the shape matches (&#96;page&#96;, &#96;results&#96;, &#96;total_results&#96;, etc.); confirm &#96;total_results&#96; for the “{n} results for '{query}'” line.\n\n## 3. Navigation: Search stack + Detail\n\nUpdate [&#96;src/navigation/types.ts&#96;](src/navigation/types.ts):\n\n- Define &#96;SearchStackParamList&#96;: &#96;SearchMain: undefined&#96;, &#96;Detail: { movieId: string }&#96; (mirror [&#96;HomeStackParamList&#96;](src/navigation/types.ts) Detail params).\n- Set &#96;SearchTab: NavigatorScreenParams\u003cSearchStackParamList\u003e&#96; (same pattern as &#96;HomeTab&#96;).\n\nUpdate [&#96;src/navigation/RootNavigator.tsx&#96;](src/navigation/RootNavigator.tsx):\n\n- Add &#96;SearchStack&#96; with &#96;SearchMain&#96; → current search UI screen component, and &#96;Detail&#96; → [&#96;DetailScreen&#96;](src/screens/DetailScreen.tsx), &#96;headerShown: false&#96; (match Home).\n- Tab &#96;SearchTab&#96; component = &#96;SearchStackNavigator&#96;, not &#96;SearchScreen&#96; directly.\n- **Hide tab bar on Search &#96;Detail&#96;**: reuse the same &#96;getFocusedRouteNameFromRoute&#96; pattern used for &#96;HomeTab&#96; / &#96;Detail&#96; (lines ~193–204), extended so when the focused route under &#96;SearchTab&#96; is &#96;Detail&#96;, &#96;tabBarStyle: { display: 'none' }&#96;.\n\nFrom Search results/trending grids: &#96;navigation.navigate('Detail', { movieId })&#96; inside the Search stack.\n\n**Watchlist** today uses &#96;navigation.navigate('HomeTab', { screen: 'Detail', params: { movieId } })&#96; ([&#96;WatchlistScreen.tsx&#96;](src/screens/WatchlistScreen.tsx)); no change required unless you want cross-tab consistency later.\n\n## 4. Hooks (core behavior)\n\n### 4a. Debounced search + cancellation (400ms)\n\nImplement in [&#96;src/hooks/useSearch.ts&#96;](src/hooks/useSearch.ts) (or split &#96;useSearchQuery.ts&#96; if file grows too large):\n\n- **State**: input text (&#96;searchText&#96;), derived UI flags, search status (&#96;idle&#96; | &#96;loading&#96; | &#96;error&#96; | &#96;success&#96;), &#96;results&#96;, &#96;totalCount&#96;, &#96;error&#96; (retry clears and re-runs).\n- **Debounce**: &#96;useRef&#96; for timeout id; on &#96;searchText&#96; change, &#96;clearTimeout&#96;, schedule **400ms** later to set “committed query” and trigger fetch. **Do not** call the API on every keystroke.\n- **Cancellation**: keep &#96;useRef\u003cAbortController | null\u003e&#96;; before starting a new request, &#96;abort()&#96; the previous controller and create a new &#96;AbortController&#96; for &#96;fetchSearchMoviesPage(..., signal)&#96;.\n- **Chip / immediate search** (spec: pre-fill + **immediate** search): expose e.g. &#96;applyGenreChip(label: string)&#96; that sets input text, **cancels pending debounce timer**, and runs the same fetch path immediately. For **&#96;All&#96;**, clear text and return to default view (no genre query)—avoid searching the literal string &#96;\"All\"&#96;.\n- **Recent searches**: on **successful** search completion for a **non-empty** trimmed query, append to recent list (dedupe, newest first, max **5**). **Not** on every keystroke.\n\n### 4b. Recent searches persistence\n\nAdd [&#96;src/store/recentSearchesStore.ts&#96;](src/store/recentSearchesStore.ts) (Zustand + &#96;persist&#96; + AsyncStorage), following [&#96;watchlistStore.ts&#96;](src/store/watchlistStore.ts):\n\n- &#96;terms: string[]&#96;, &#96;hydrated&#96;, &#96;addTerm&#96;, &#96;clearAll&#96;.\n- &#96;partialize&#96; to &#96;terms&#96; only; merge/rehydrate safely.\n- **Clear All** calls &#96;clearAll&#96; and updates UI immediately.\n\n### 4c. Default “Trending Now” data\n\nNew hook e.g. [&#96;src/hooks/useSearchTrending.ts&#96;](src/hooks/useSearchTrending.ts):\n\n- Load [&#96;fetchTrendingMoviesPage(1)&#96;](src/api/movies.ts) (or reuse existing export).\n- **Featured** (first result): large landscape card with **“FEATURED”** badge using &#96;colors.secondary_container&#96; (spec differs from [&#96;HeroFeaturedCard&#96;](src/components/home/HeroFeaturedCard.tsx) “NEW RELEASE” / primary badge).\n- **Metadata line** “genre • year • runtime”: &#96;MovieSummary&#96; from trending list does not include genres/runtime. Use [&#96;fetchMovieById&#96;](src/api/movies.ts) for the **featured** id only (one extra request), then format genres + [&#96;releaseYear&#96;](src/utils/watchlistItem.ts) + runtime.\n- **Grid**: remaining items from page 1 (e.g. index 1..n) in a **2-column** portrait grid with **rating badge** (same visual language as [&#96;WatchlistGridCard&#96;](src/components/watchlist/WatchlistGridCard.tsx) rating, without remove affordance).\n\nLoading/error/empty: skeletons + retry consistent with other screens ([&#96;DetailSectionError&#96;](src/components/detail/DetailSectionError.tsx) pattern or local error row).\n\n## 5. UI components (presentation only)\n\nPlace under &#96;src/components/search/&#96; (matches &#96;home/&#96;, &#96;watchlist/&#96;):\n\n| Piece | Notes |\n|-------|--------|\n| **Header** | “StreamList” wordmark + circular **avatar placeholder** (top right)—reuse typography/colors from [&#96;HomeHeader&#96;](src/components/home/HomeHeader.tsx) brand row; replace bell with &#96;account-circle-outline&#96; or similar. |\n| **Search bar** | &#96;surface_container_low&#96; fill, **12px radius** → add a named token in [&#96;layout.ts&#96;](src/theme/layout.ts) (e.g. &#96;searchBarRadius&#96;) to avoid magic numbers. Left magnifier icon; placeholder *\"Search movies, actors, directors...\"*; focused border: &#96;outline_variant&#96; (already 15% white in [&#96;colors.ts&#96;](src/theme/colors.ts)). |\n| **Genre strip** | Reuse [&#96;GenreFilterStrip&#96;](src/components/home/GenreFilterStrip.tsx) **styles**; behavior via wrapper or thin &#96;SearchGenreChips&#96; that calls hook &#96;applyGenreChip&#96; / &#96;clearForAll&#96; without a persistent “selected” index (all chips can use the inactive surface style, or only highlight nothing). |\n| **Recent** | Section title &#96;headline-md&#96;; **CLEAR ALL** right-aligned, &#96;title-sm&#96; + uppercase, &#96;primary_container&#96;. Rows: clock icon + term &#96;body-md&#96; &#96;on_surface&#96;. **Visibility**: show only when &#96;searchText === ''&#96; **and** search input **not focused** (local &#96;onFocus&#96;/&#96;onBlur&#96;). |\n| **Results** | &#96;{n} results for '{query}'&#96; with &#96;label-sm&#96; &#96;on_surface_variant&#96;. **2-column** grid: poster, &#96;title-lg&#96;, year &#96;label-sm&#96; &#96;on_surface_variant&#96;, rating badge top-right. |\n| **Zero results** | Centered icon, “No results for '{query}'” &#96;headline-md&#96;, supporting copy &#96;body-md&#96; &#96;on_surface_variant&#96;. |\n| **Skeletons** | Match project rule: skeleton loaders, not spinners, for loading states. |\n\n[&#96;ContentCard&#96;](src/components/common/ContentCard.tsx) lacks year + rating; add **&#96;SearchResultCard&#96;** (or **&#96;SearchGridCell&#96;**) rather than overloading &#96;ContentCard&#96; for this screen.\n\n## 6. Theming compliance\n\n- Colors/spacing/typography only from [&#96;colors.ts&#96;](src/theme/colors.ts), [&#96;spacing.ts&#96;](src/theme/spacing.ts), [&#96;typography.ts&#96;](src/theme/typography.ts) (e.g. &#96;headline-md&#96;, &#96;title-lg&#96;, &#96;body-md&#96;, &#96;label-sm&#96;).\n- **CLEAR ALL** uses uppercase via &#96;textTransform: 'uppercase'&#96; with &#96;title-sm&#96;.\n\n## 7. Tests\n\n- Extend or add tests under &#96;__tests__/&#96;: e.g. unit tests for **recent search store** (add/dedupe/max 5/clear) and/or **debounce + abort** logic using Jest fake timers and mocked API.\n- Update [&#96;__tests__/App.test.tsx&#96;](__tests__/App.test.tsx) only if imports break.\n\n## 8. Verification checklist (matches “What Good Looks Like”)\n\n- Type rapidly: only the latest query’s results appear; network shows aborted/cancelled prior calls (dev tools / logging in dev only—**no** secrets logged).\n- Debounce ~400ms after typing stops.\n- Recent searches survive app restart (AsyncStorage via persist).\n- Genre chips fill the bar and trigger search (**All** clears to trending).\n- Empty query shows default + recent (when allowed); non-empty shows results line + grid or zero state.\n- Tapping a movie opens **Detail** within Search stack with back returning to Search.\n",
  "todos": [

_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Implementing store**

I'm implementing recentSearchesStore with persist middleware for term storage. I'm adding the `addTerm` and `clearAll` actions to manage recent searches in the store. I'm setting hydrated to false initially and updating it to true on rehydrate for Zustand persist. 

I'm implementing the search hook with debouncing and abort error handling for the movie search functionality. I'm adding error handling utilities for cancellable requests in the search hook. I'm implementing the search hook state management with debouncing and error handling. 

I'm adding debounced search functionality with abortable requests and recent searches integration. I'm resetting search state and setting up abortable request for search functionality. 

**Handling search errors**

I'm implementing abortable search requests with proper error handling for cancelled requests. I'm adding debounce logic to search requests and cancelling in-flight queries on text input changes. I'm refactoring the search effect to prevent conflicts between debounced searches and immediate clears. 

I'm clearing debounce and running search immediately in applyGenreChip to handle genre selection without delay. I'm splitting the debounce logic to handle immediate chip queries separately from typing debounces. I'm refactoring debounce logic to handle chip selections without unnecessary delays. 

I'm using a ref to track debounce state to prevent duplicate fetches when chips are selected. I'm refactoring the debounce logic to use onChangeText handler instead of useEffect for better control over search requests. 

**Refactoring debounce logic**

I'm replacing the useEffect debounce with onChangeText handler to avoid duplicate search calls. I'm using clearTimeout and abort pattern to prevent stale search calls, ensuring runSearch uses latest trimmed query from closure. I'm implementing conditional addTerm calls based on fetch success and handling empty query states correctly. I'm using `activeQuery` only for results mode during debounce, showing loading indicator on next fetch or keeping old results. 

I'm simplifying the debounce logic to use `committedQuery` for tracking the last successful fetch, showing loading state only when needed. I'm updating the UI state logic to show results when `searchText.trim().length > 0`, incorporating the debounced query behavior. I'm implementing the display logic for debounced search results, ensuring `searchText.trim()` is used for the result count while `activeQuery` updates only after fetch completion. I'm tracking `submittedQuery` as the query last requested from API and updating `resultsQuery` on fetch start for accurate result count labeling. 

**Updating search labels**

I'm showing loading state when `searchText.trim() !== submittedQuery` to avoid displaying stale results with outdated labels. I'm simplifying the query handling by using `effectiveSearchQuery` state that updates when fetches begin, ensuring labels match loading results. I'm implementing `runFetch` to handle search queries with proper abort controller usage and status updates. 

**Handling search input**

I'm implementing debounced search with clearTimeout and setSearchText updates. I'm using trim() on search text to ensure empty queries are handled properly. I'm implementing debouncing with useRef to capture the latest search text for timeout closures. </details></think>

_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_


Here’s what was implemented and how it fits together.

### API
- [`src/api/client.ts`](src/api/client.ts): `getJson` accepts optional `{ signal?: AbortSignal }` and passes it to Axios.
- [`src/api/types.ts`](src/api/types.ts): `TmdbMovieSearchResult`, `MovieSearchHit`, `PaginatedSearchMoviesResponse`.
- [`src/api/movies.ts`](src/api/movies.ts): `fetchSearchMoviesPage`, exported `mapMovie`.

### Navigation
- [`src/navigation/types.ts`](src/navigation/types.ts): `SearchStackParamList` with `SearchMain` + `Detail`; `SearchTab` uses nested params.
- [`src/navigation/RootNavigator.tsx`](src/navigation/RootNavigator.tsx): `SearchStackNavigator` wrapping `SearchScreen` and `DetailScreen`; tab bar hidden on Search `Detail` like Home.

### State & hooks
- [`src/store/recentSearchesStore.ts`](src/store/recentSearchesStore.ts): Zustand + persist, max 5 terms, case-insensitive dedupe, `clearAll`.
- [`src/hooks/useSearch.ts`](src/hooks/useSearch.ts): 400 ms debounce via `setTimeout` + ref, `AbortController` per request, generation guard, `applyGenreChip` ( **`All`** clears), `applySearchQuery` for recent rows, recent terms saved only after a **successful** fetch.
- [`src/hooks/useSearchTrending.ts`](src/hooks/useSearchTrending.ts): Trending page 1, `fetchMovieById` for featured metadata, grid from remaining items.

### UI (`src/components/search/`)
- Header, bar (`layout.searchBarRadius` in [`src/theme/layout.ts`](src/theme/layout.ts)), genre chips, recent block, trending featured + grid, result cards, zero state, skeletons.
- [`src/screens/SearchScreen.tsx`](src/screens/SearchScreen.tsx): Default scroll (recent + trending) vs results `FlatList` with header; no fetch logic in the screen.

### Other fixes
- [`src/screens/DetailScreen.tsx`](src/screens/DetailScreen.tsx): “See all” for similar uses the **tab** navigator to open `HomeTab` → `SeeAll` so it works from Search’s stack; `push` for similar titles cast for typing.
- [`src/screens/WatchlistScreen.tsx`](src/screens/WatchlistScreen.tsx): `navigate('SearchTab', { screen: 'SearchMain' })`.

### Tests
- [`__tests__/recentSearchesStore.test.ts`](__tests__/recentSearchesStore.test.ts): Dedupe, cap at 5, clear.
- [`__tests__/useSearch.test.tsx`](__tests__/useSearch.test.tsx): Debounced call with `AbortSignal`.

`yarn test` and `npx tsc --noEmit` pass. `yarn lint` still fails on the existing `jest/globals` ESLint environment issue in this repo, not on these changes.

---

_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_


Here’s what changed:

### 1. Genre chips — selected state (matches Home)
- [`SearchGenreChips.tsx`](src/components/search/SearchGenreChips.tsx) now takes a **`selectedLabel`** prop and uses the same styling as [`GenreFilterStrip`](src/components/home/GenreFilterStrip.tsx): active = `secondary_container` + `on_surface`, inactive = `surface_container_high` + `on_surface_variant`, plus `accessibilityState.selected`.
- [`SearchScreen.tsx`](src/screens/SearchScreen.tsx) derives selection with `useMemo`:
  - **Empty** search → **`'All'`** is selected.
  - **Exact match** to a chip label (e.g. `Action` after tapping that chip) → that chip stays highlighted.
  - **Free text** that doesn’t match any chip → **`null`** → all chips use the inactive style.

### 2. Recent searches — always visible on default + Clear all
- The **Recent Searches** block shows whenever you’re on the default view (no query), storage is hydrated, and the field isn’t focused — **even if the list is empty**.
- **With items**: rows + **CLEAR ALL** (unchanged behavior).
- **Empty**: headline **Recent Searches** plus hint copy: *No recent searches yet.* (no CLEAR ALL).

Files touched: [`SearchGenreChips.tsx`](src/components/search/SearchGenreChips.tsx), [`SearchScreen.tsx`](src/screens/SearchScreen.tsx), [`SearchRecentSection.tsx`](src/components/search/SearchRecentSection.tsx). `tsc` and tests pass.

Here’s what was implemented:

### Icons (white)
- Back and share use `colors.icon_on_dark` (`#FFFFFF` in [`src/theme/colors.ts`](src/theme/colors.ts)), matching the spec for icons on dark hero/header areas.

### Share behavior
- New [`src/utils/shareMovie.ts`](src/utils/shareMovie.ts) uses React Native’s built-in [`Share`](https://reactnative.dev/docs/share) API so the system sheet lists WhatsApp, Messages, Instagram, Facebook, etc.
- **Shared text includes:**
  - Movie **title**
  - **Tagline** (when TMDB provides one)
  - **Overview** (trimmed, max ~280 characters)
  - **Poster** line with the TMDB image URL (`Poster: https://…`)
  - **More:** link to `https://www.themoviedb.org/movie/{id}`
- **iOS:** `url` is set to the poster URL when available so apps that support a link preview can use it together with `message`.
- **Android:** Everything is in `message` (including the poster URL), which is what most apps expect.

### Data model
- **`tagline`** is read from TMDB on [`TmdbMovieDetailResult`](src/api/types.ts) and mapped on [`MovieDetail`](src/api/types.ts) in [`mapMovieDetail`](src/api/movies.ts).

### Detail screen
- Share runs only when details have loaded (`canShare`); while loading/error, the control is disabled and slightly faded.
- [`showShareErrorToast`](src/utils/toast.ts) if sharing fails.

### Tests
- [`__tests__/shareMovie.test.ts`](__tests__/shareMovie.test.ts) covers `buildMovieShareMessage` (with `toast` mocked so Jest doesn’t load `react-native-toast-message`).

**Note:** Attaching the poster as a **binary image** to WhatsApp usually needs downloading the file and using a native share module. This implementation shares **text + poster URL + TMDB page**, which is the standard approach with core React Native and still pastes cleanly into WhatsApp with title, tagline, and links.

---

_**User**_

in @src/screens/HomeScreen.tsx fix the filter if select any filter item like action, dram, comedy then show the content according to filter in homescreen and @src/screens/DetailScreen.tsx share functionality should be improve need to ahow image preview after sharing it to another application like social media

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Optimizing discover hook**

I'll keep the discover hook always running but optimize it to only fetch when selectedGenreId is null. I'm optimizing the discover hook to avoid wasteful API calls by using a single hook that skips fetching when a genre is selected. I'm implementing the skip pattern to avoid unnecessary API calls when a genre is selected. I'm using a single discover hook with a dynamic listKey to handle genre selection without breaking React's hook rules. 

I'm adjusting the discover hook to use a hidden list key when a genre is selected to prevent unnecessary Action discover loads. I'm adding an `enabled` flag to `usePaginatedMovies` to prevent fetches when genre is selected. I'm refactoring the discover hook to use a single fetch with conditional filtering instead of multiple fetches. I'm removing the discover hook entirely and splitting `HomeDiscoverRow` into an inner component that only mounts when `selectedGenreId===null`. </details></think>

_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_


Extracting the Popular row into a child component so we don't run a redundant discover fetch when a genre is selected.

_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_


Here’s what was implemented:

### Home screen genre filter ([`src/screens/HomeScreen.tsx`](src/screens/HomeScreen.tsx))
- **Trending** and **Top Rated** now follow the selected chip:
  - **All**: same as before (global trending + global top rated).
  - **Specific genre**: TMDB **discover** with `with_genres`, `popularity.desc` for Trending and `vote_average.desc` + `vote_count.gte=200` for Top Rated ([`src/hooks/usePaginatedMovies.ts`](src/hooks/usePaginatedMovies.ts), [`src/api/movies.ts`](src/api/movies.ts)).
- **Hero** updates with the chip via [`useHomeHero(selectedGenreId)`](src/hooks/useHomeHero.ts): global trending hero for **All**, top title in that genre for a selected genre ([`fetchHeroFromDiscoverGenre`](src/api/movies.ts)).
- **Popular** row only shows for **All** (implemented as [`HomePopularRow`](src/screens/HomeScreen.tsx) so its pagination hook does not run while a genre is selected—avoids duplicate “popular” lists and extra API calls).
- **See All** passes `genreId` for Trending and Top Rated when a genre is selected so [`SeeAllScreen`](src/screens/SeeAllScreen.tsx) stays in sync.

### Discover API ([`src/api/movies.ts`](src/api/movies.ts))
- `fetchDiscoverMoviesPage` accepts optional `DiscoverMoviesOptions`: `sortBy`, `voteCountGte`.

### Share with image preview ([`src/utils/shareMovie.ts`](src/utils/shareMovie.ts))
- Added **`react-native-share`** (native share sheet with image) and **`react-native-blob-util`** (download poster to cache).
- Flow: download poster JPEG → open share with **`urls: [file]`** + **message** (title, tagline, synopsis excerpt, TMDB link) so apps like WhatsApp can show the **image** and text.
- If download/share fails, falls back to the built-in `Share` API (text only).
- No poster path → text-only share as before.

### Dependencies & iOS
- `yarn add react-native-share react-native-blob-util` (see [`package.json`](package.json)).
- `pod install` was run so **iOS** picks up the new pods.

After pulling, run **`cd ios && pod install`** if needed, rebuild the app, and test share on a device/simulator—WhatsApp/Instagram behavior can vary slightly by OS version.

_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_

Here’s what was fixed and why it helps on **iOS and Android** (including small / wide devices).

### Shared: touch targets & layout
- Added [`layout.minTouchTarget`](src/theme/layout.ts) (**48**) for consistent primary button height (accessibility-friendly on both platforms).

### Detail – Add to Watchlist ([`DetailWatchlistButton.tsx`](src/components/detail/DetailWatchlistButton.tsx))
- **Full-width** actions: wrapper + buttons use `width: '100%'` so the gradient state isn’t narrower than the “In Watchlist” outline state (a common iOS issue when only one side had `width: '100%'`).
- **Gradient path**: `Pressable` uses `overflow: 'hidden'` + full width; `LinearGradient` fills with `minHeight: layout.minTouchTarget`, centered row content.
- Replaced **`gap`** with **`leadIcon` `marginRight`** so icon/text spacing is stable everywhere.
- **`numberOfLines={1}`** + **`flexShrink: 1`** on labels to avoid awkward wraps on narrow widths.
- Skeleton uses the same **min height** as the real buttons.

### Home – Watch Now / Details ([`HeroFeaturedCard.tsx`](src/components/home/HeroFeaturedCard.tsx))
- **Row actions**: `alignItems: 'stretch'`, `width: '100%'`, **`minWidth: 0`** on both `flex: 1` halves so iOS lays out the row correctly and text doesn’t overflow.
- **Spacing between buttons**: `marginRight` on Watch (not `gap`) for reliable layout.
- **Watch Now**: `LinearGradient` uses **`flex: 1`**, **`minHeight: layout.minTouchTarget`**, icon uses **`watchIcon`** margin (no `gap`).
- **Details**: matching **`minHeight`**, **`minWidth: 0`**, label **`numberOfLines={1}`**.
- **Inner** content: `width: '100%'` so the action row matches card width.
- **Skeleton** action placeholders aligned with the same flex / margin / min-height pattern.

### Detail screen container ([`DetailScreen.tsx`](src/screens/DetailScreen.tsx))
- **`padded`**: `width: '100%'`, `maxWidth: '100%'`, `alignSelf: 'stretch'` so the watchlist button and text block use the full scroll width on all devices.

`yarn test` and `tsc` both pass. If anything still looks off on a specific device, say which model/OS and we can tune padding or font scaling next.

_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Fixing text truncation**

I'm addressing the ContentCard title truncation issue caused by improper width constraints and text layout differences between iOS and Android. The title truncation issue stems from potential clipping at the start of text due to transform, textAlign, or padding issues on the parent element. The text truncation issue likely stems from overflow: hidden on a parent View causing iOS to clip text from the left, or incorrect width calculations making the text area appear wider than its container. 

**Investigating text truncation**

The text truncation issue appears to be caused by flex layout and maxWidth interactions, where 'Avatar' becomes 'atar' due to missing first two characters, likely from padding or margin misalignment. The text truncation issue stems from iOS's textAlign center behavior causing clipping with certain fonts when combined with Pressable's width styling. 

**Adjusting text layout**

I'm adding `width: '100%'` to the title text style to fix truncation issues. I'm adding `ellipsizeMode: 'tail'` for iOS text truncation. I'm investigating why the Browse Trending Now text is invisible on iOS, considering potential issues with text color, font scaling, or z-index ordering. 

**Fixing ios layout**

I'm wrapping the CTA content in a View with flexDirection row and align center to fix the iOS LinearGradient layout issue. I'm adjusting the padding top in the empty state to account for safe area insets. 

**Adjusting ios layout**

I'm ensuring the empty state padding accounts for safe area insets by applying `paddingTop: insets.top` to the root view. I'm aligning the headerBlock padding with the WatchlistHeader to fix inconsistent left margins. I'm adjusting the trending scroll layout to ensure consistent horizontal padding by removing left padding from the first poster item. I'm fixing iOS ScrollView padding issue by adding paddingRight to trending scroll end and ensuring contentContainerStyle has proper padding. I'm adjusting the horizontal ScrollView's left padding to align the first card with the 'POPULAR' text using the parent's contentContainerStyle paddingHorizontal spacing. I'm fixing the Popular recommendations misalignment by ensuring consistent width and alignItems styling across the header block and label. 

**Fixing ios layout**

I'm adding an inner View with width 100%, alignItems center, and minHeight to fix iOS hitSlop issue with LinearGradient children. I'm adding zIndex: 1 to Text and position: relative on gradient to fix iOS rendering issues. 

**Debugging text rendering**

I'm investigating a text truncation issue on iOS 18 where fontWeight: '600' with numberOfLines={2} causes rendering glitches. I'm adjusting the title text styles with explicit width and alignSelf to fix iOS 18 truncation issues. 

**Adjusting CTA styles**

I'm increasing CTA minHeight and adding flex to gradient wrapper to ensure text visibility. </details></think>

_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_

Here’s what was finished for the **iOS alignment** issues from your screenshots (and why).

### 1. Home hero — **“Watch Now”** (`HeroFeaturedCard.tsx`)
**Issue:** Icon + label sat directly in `LinearGradient`, which on iOS often lays out children oddly, so the row looked vertically off and cramped.

**Change:** The gradient is now a **fill layer** (`watchGradientFill`), and the play icon + **“Watch Now”** sit in a nested row (`watchGradientRow`) with explicit horizontal padding and centered alignment. That matches how flex layouts behave on iOS vs Android.

### 2. Watchlist empty — **blank red CTA bar** (`WatchlistEmptyState.tsx`)
**Issue:** The gradient sometimes didn’t give the label a proper box, so on iOS the text could disappear or not lay out.

**Change:** Wrapped the label in a full-width inner **`View`** (`ctaInner`) with padding, set **`minHeight`** on the gradient (`ctaGradient`) to match the touch target, and moved border radius + **`overflow: 'hidden'`** to the **`Pressable`** (`ctaPressable`) so the tap target and visuals stay aligned. **`numberOfLines={1}`** + **`textAlign: 'center'`** on the label avoids odd clipping.

Also **`headerBlock`** / **`popularLabel`** use **`width: '100%'`** so horizontal alignment matches the padded scroll content, and the horizontal list no longer relies on **`gap`** (uses **`marginRight`** on **`trendingCardWrap`** only), which is safer for older RN / iOS layout.

### 3. Trending row titles (`ContentCard.tsx`)
Already addressed earlier: fixed column width, **`ellipsizeMode="tail"`**, and **`lineHeight: 20`** for Inter + narrow columns on iOS.

---

**Checks:** `yarn tsc --noEmit` and `yarn test` both pass.

**What you should do:** Run the iOS simulator again and confirm: Trending titles ellipsis cleanly, hero **Watch Now** looks centered, and Watchlist empty shows **“Browse Trending Now”** on the gradient. Android should remain unchanged in a good way; if anything regresses there, say which screen.
